entities.name: PARTICIPATORY VARIETY SELECTION
entities.description: An approach providing farmers a wide choice of varieties to evaluate in their own environment to increase production and adoption.

entities.name: FINGER MILLET
entities.description: The crop variety being introduced, evaluated, and released in Tanzania.

entities.name: SINGIDA
entities.description: A district in Central Tanzania where PVS was implemented.

entities.name: IRAMBA
entities.description: A district in Central Tanzania where PVS was implemented.

entities.name: CENTRAL TANZANIA
entities.description: The region where the study was conducted.

entities.name: NORTHERN TANZANIA
entities.description: A region where finger millet varieties were promoted for adoption.

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: A goal ensured by the dissemination and adoption of diverse crop varieties.

entities.name: COMMUNITY SEED PRODUCTION
entities.description: A process promoted by the PVS approach.

entities.name: COMMUNITY SEED BANKS
entities.description: Facilities promoted by the PVS approach for seed storage and access.

entities.name: LAND RACES
entities.description: Traditional varieties grown by farmers, characterized as low yielding and susceptible to drought and disease.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: An agronomic trait focused on increasing production, with farmers preferring higher yield.

entities.name: MATURITY
entities.description: The duration for a crop to mature, with land races being long maturing and women preferring short duration.

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: An environmental stressor that land races were susceptible to.

entities.name: DISEASE
entities.description: A biological stressor that land races were susceptible to.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: A risk-averting trait preferred by women farmers.

entities.name: DISEASE RESISTANCE
entities.description: A risk-averting trait preferred by women farmers.

entities.name: COMPACT HEADS
entities.description: A physical trait preferred by women farmers.

entities.name: BROWN COLOUR
entities.description: A market-related trait preferred by male farmers.

entities.name: BIG HEAD
entities.description: A market-related trait preferred by male farmers.

entities.name: U15
entities.description: One of the first finger millet varieties released in Tanzania through PVS.

entities.name: P224
entities.description: One of the first finger millet varieties released in Tanzania through PVS.

entities.name: QUALITY DECLARED SEED
entities.description: Affordable high quality seed produced by target farmer groups.

entities.name: FARMERS
entities.description: The stakeholders who evaluate, select, and adopt crop varieties.
